user_query:
I’m an HR librarian building a curated reading list for our software engineers on leadership, interpersonal skills, and HR best practices. Please use the input file at input/domains.yml to drive targeted Internet searches and produce a small, verifiable collection. Deliverables and constraints:

Inputs:
- Read input/domains.yml for the official organizations, domain patterns, topics, and minimum counts. Use those domains and topics to form site-restricted search engine queries.

Required steps and outputs:
1) Perform search engine queries limited to the official domains listed in input/domains.yml, combining the domain pattern with relevant topic keywords from the file. Record every query you run and the engine used in workspace/logs/queries.md (include timestamps and the organization/topic each query targeted).
2) Select at least 6 publicly accessible resource pages (no logins/paywalls) that match the topics and minimum-by-organization constraints from input/domains.yml. For each selected page, extract structured metadata from the page content and save a CSV to workspace/output/resources.csv with exactly these fields:
   - source_org (use the organization key from the input file, e.g., SHRM/HBR/CIPD)
   - domain (the hostname of the page you selected)
   - url (full canonical URL of the resource page)
   - page_title (from the page’s HTML <title> or meta og:title; indicate which you used in extraction_method)
   - resource_type (e.g., article, report, book, guide, event; infer from the page)
   - publication_year (if explicitly stated on the page; blank if not present)
   - tags (semicolon-separated; choose from tags_allowed in input/domains.yml that best fit the resource; include “software engineering” if the resource explicitly addresses engineers)
   - relevance_note (one sentence on why this helps software engineers grow)
   - robots_txt_status_code (HTTP status code when fetching robots.txt for that domain)
   - robots_txt_disallow_paths (semicolon-separated summary of any Disallow lines that might affect scraping of article pages on that domain; leave blank if none)
   - resource_fetch_status_code (HTTP status code when fetching the resource page)
   - extraction_method (e.g., html-title, meta-og:title)
   Use only pages from the domain_pattern values in input/domains.yml. If a candidate page is off-domain or requires login, exclude it.
3) Create a summary JSON at workspace/output/summary.json with: total resource count, counts by source_org, counts by resource_type, and a sorted list of distinct tags used.
4) Use a command-line HTTP client (e.g., curl or equivalent) to fetch robots.txt for each selected domain and to test-fetch each resource page. Save the exact commands you ran and their outputs (including HTTP status lines and any error messages) to workspace/logs/commands.log. If any command fails or returns non-200, include the error output and a short note explaining how you handled it (e.g., excluded the page, retried, or accepted read-only access).
5) Draft an email to our engineering leads summarizing your top 3 recommended readings from the CSV (include titles, links, and a short rationale for each), plus a brief note on access/usage considerations and whether robots.txt suggests any constraints on automated retrieval. Save as workspace/output/email_to_eng_leads.txt.
6) Create workspace/output/validation.txt containing: (a) a one-line shell command you ran locally to verify the CSV header and count data rows (use a standard tool of your choice, e.g., awk, python -c, or csvkit), and (b) the exact output of that command. If the command produced warnings or errors, briefly analyze the cause and how you addressed it.

Constraints:
- Honor the minimum counts per organization from input/domains.yml (at least 2 from SHRM, 2 from HBR, 1 from CIPD).
- Only use pages from the official domains listed; do not use mirrors or aggregator sites.
- Avoid paywalled or login-required pages.
- The Internet search step is required; do not fabricate data.

Verification:
- Outputs are verified by the existence and structure of the artifacts: queries log, CSV with specified fields, summary JSON, commands log with HTTP outputs, email draft, and validation.txt with a command and its output.

Let me know if anything in input/domains.yml seems unclear before you start. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
